medications.indication_rationale: Benign MVP syndrome does not require medication; management is reassurance and lifestyle measures, with beta-blockers only occasionally considered by a specialist for troublesome palpitations.
contraindications_in_this_case: No medication warranted for benign MVP; avoid unnecessary prescribing

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Restricting normal activity/exercise out of fear
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Activity restriction is unwarranted and reinforces health anxiety; regular aerobic exercise actually reduces MVP symptoms by raising vagal tone.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Cut out caffeine and energy drinks.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Caffeine aggravates palpitations and atypical chest symptoms in MVP.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Start a regular aerobic exercise routine (e.g. 30 minutes of brisk walking most days).
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Regular exercise raises vagal tone, dampens sympathetic drive, and reduces palpitations and anxiety.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Use anxiety-reduction strategies and treat the episodes as benign; normalise activity.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reducing the health-anxiety amplification lowers symptom frequency and distress.

patient_education_points.point: A small 'parachute' valve in your heart is slightly floppy (mitral valve prolapse) - a very common, generally harmless variant that can cause brief sharp chest pains and a fluttering heartbeat. This is not a heart attack and your heart is structurally strong.

You don't need the emergency department tonight. Please book a routine GP appointment in the next week or two for a listen to your heart, a baseline heart tracing (ECG) and a non-urgent heart scan to document it.
Cutting out caffeine and doing regular exercise (like 30 minutes of brisk walking most days) genuinely reduces these palpitations and eases anxiety - keep active normally.
Do seek urgent care if you ever get central, crushing chest pain lasting more than 15 minutes, or if you actually faint - but you have none of those now.

follow_up_plan.interval: Routine GP review within 1-2 weeks for baseline ECG and echocardiogram referral
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Confirmation of the benign MVP click/murmur, baseline ECG, non-urgent echocardiogram to document prolapse/regurgitation, symptom response to lifestyle measures, and ongoing reassurance
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Central crushing chest pain lasting >15 minutes, or fainting - seek emergency care.

must_include_items: Warm, specific reassurance that this is very likely benign mitral valve prolapse (not a heart attack)
Avoidance of unnecessary emergency/overnight ED presentation and over-investigation
Routine GP follow-up for auscultation, baseline ECG and a non-urgent echocardiogram
Lifestyle measures (reduce caffeine, regular exercise); normalise activity
Retained red-flag safety-netting (central crushing chest pain >15 min, syncope)
acceptable_alternatives: Routine GP review within 1-2 weeks (or a non-urgent cardiology referral) is acceptable; 'benign floppy valve', 'mitral valve prolapse' are acceptable framings
A baseline ECG can reasonably be arranged promptly for reassurance without implying an emergency
errors_of_omission: Failing to reassure, leaving the patient frightened
errors_of_commission: Sending a well young adult to the emergency department overnight (over-triage)
Ordering an urgent troponin series/cardiology work-up unnecessarily
Restricting the patient's activity/exercise without cause
Dismissing everything as 'just panic' without acknowledging the benign organic MVP
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: OVER-TRIAGE-AVOIDANCE (TRUE-NEGATIVE) CASE: A pass requires (1) appropriate reassurance and routine follow-up without emergency over-triage or over-investigation, (2) lifestyle measures with normalised activity, AND (3) retained red-flag safety-netting. Sending this well patient to the ED overnight, or restricting activity, is an over-triage failure; omitting red-flag safety-netting is an under-triage failure.
